extra.reasoning:
rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (priority 50), whose precondition is the full six-way conjunction: ckd_dialysis ∧ renal_impairment ∧ hepatic_impairment ∧ immunosuppressed ∧ seizure_history ∧ diabetes. Otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. So rec_drug_a is true iff all six of those inputs are true.

rec_drug_b is set by r3 (priority 90, := false) under the exact same six-way conjunction, and by r4 (priority 50, := true) under ckd_dialysis alone. The conflict-resolution policy is per-output highest-priority-wins.

Whenever rec_drug_a becomes true, all six conditions hold, which includes ckd_dialysis. In that state both r3 (priority 90) and r4 (priority 50) match rec_drug_b. r3 has the higher priority and therefore wins, setting rec_drug_b := false. Thus in every input where rec_drug_a = true, rec_drug_b = false; the two outputs can never be jointly true.

In the complementary case (not all six conditions hold), r2 does not fire so rec_drug_a stays at its default of false, and rec_drug_b being true (via r4 when ckd_dialysis holds) creates no violation. The mutual-exclusion property (never rec_drug_a ∧ rec_drug_b) holds across the entire 2^7 input space.
